title: GMM Persistent-State Schema (gmm-persistent-state-v1.0)
description: Schema for the GMM (Global Macro Monitor / macro-monitor) `persistent-state.json` — the canonical knowledge-base file the Applier writes to. The MVP shape is intentionally permissive on per-key structure (`additionalProperties: true`); per-key strictness is the responsibility of future MBC sweeps. The strict layer is the `_meta` block — `schema_version`, `last_updated`, `last_issue`, `monitor_slug`. Sprint-5 Phase 3 (BRIEF cf14eabd, E10 cure) adds a top-level `arrays` block declaring, for every persistent-state array GMM cares about, the merge `key_field` and a `key_constraint` (regex pattern OR string enum). The engine (`pipeline.engine.persistent_state.apply_patches_to_persistent_state`, amended in Phase 1) reads these declarations to enforce additive merge semantics and raise `DisjointKeySchemeError` on key-scheme rotation. GMM green-run acceptance target: Tue 2026-05-19.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this file. New canonical value: `gmm-persistent-state-v1.0`. Legacy bake value `2.0` (string or number) is accepted during the AD-2026-04-26Y bake window.

last_updated.description: Most recent material state change. Either ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) or full ISO-8601 timestamp. Bumped by the Applier on cycles that land at least one patch; NOT bumped on null cycles.

last_issue.description: Issue counter. The Applier increments this by 1 on cycles that land at least one patch when the current value is an integer; left untouched otherwise.

asset_class_baseline.describe: Per-asset-class baseline and current-state rows. `asset_class` is a closed taxonomy — enum-locked to prevent the LLM from rotating to free-form names mid-cycle. Live state carries: Metals, Energy, Bonds, Crypto, Tech, Consumer Staples, EM Equities, Real Estate. Broader canonical taxonomy extended to include Equities, Commodities, FX.

active_tactical_alerts.describe: Named tactical alert conditions with traffic-light state. `alert_id` uses the ALERT-NNN scheme in live state (ALERT-001, ALERT-002, ALERT-003). Pattern lock prevents LLM from rotating to free-form IDs or alternate zero-padding.

blind_spot_overrides.describe: Analyst blind-spot override rules. `indicator` is the machine-readable indicator name (e.g. earnings_revisions, m2_money_supply). Free-form pattern — indicator vocabulary may expand.

conviction_history.describe: Chronological weekly conviction assessments — append only. `date` is ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD). One entry per cycle; the engine treats this as additive-only by construction.

blind_spots.describe: Identified structural gaps in data coverage or analytical scope. `id` uses the BS<N> scheme in live state (BS5, BS6, BS7, BS8). Pattern lock prevents free-form id rotation.

calibration_log.describe: Append-only calibration log (one entry per material methodology change). `date` is ISO date or full ISO-8601 timestamp — the engine treats this array as additive-only by construction.

gaps_register.describe: Open-findings register. `gap_id` uses the GAP-NNN scheme in live state (GAP-001 … GAP-004). Pattern lock prevents free-form gap_id rotation.

jurisdiction_risk_matrix.describe: Per-jurisdiction macro-risk matrix declared in the interpreter-schema. `jurisdiction` is free-form (e.g. US, Euro Area, China, Japan, UK, EM). ISO-3166 normalisation deferred; free-form pattern accepted.

key_judgments.describe: GMM key-judgments array declared in the interpreter-schema. `id` pattern-locked against GMM-KJ-NNN scheme to prevent LLM from rotating to alternate id formats.
